issues: Likely runtime regression with torch.compile: run_single_trial/run_preflight access model.head, model.whiten, model.reset, and model.init_whiten directly, but after model = torch.compile(model) the object is an OptimizedModule wrapper and these attributes may not be accessible as expected; this matches the observed runtime_error on a GEPA run.
No demonstrated improvement over baseline: reported accuracy remains 0.9396999, still below the 0.94 target, so the candidate does not satisfy the objective.

Warmup reuses the same compiled model object and mutates internal state across trials; if compile succeeds, wrapper/state interactions can be brittle and are a repeated failure mode for benchmark code expecting the original nn.Module API.

Optimizer parameter selection is performed after possible compilation via model.parameters()/named_parameters(); with compiled wrappers this can be fragile and may miss assumptions about module naming like filtering names containing "norm".

Contract risk: preflight and training both rely on direct module mutation (copying model.head.weight.data, calling model.zero_grad, resetting parameters) that is safe on raw nn.Module but not guaranteed on compiled wrappers.
summary: Do not approve: accuracy change is negligible, and there is a likely hard runtime break when using torch.compile because compiled models do not preserve direct submodule access expected by training code.
